title: a11y Command Cheatsheet - Operator View for Claude and Codex
desc: Operational cheatsheet for wkappbot a11y in Claude and Codex workflows. Covers principles (inspect first, resolve grap safely, prefer focusless tiers) AND a per-sub-action reference for all 24 a11y actions. Treat find output as paste-ready contract. Include optional operator flags only when they improve safety or verification.
steps: Always inspect before automating an unfamiliar app; use find to discover not guess
Treat a11y find as a contract: one shared focus line one target block per match optional info lines shell-safe grap and verify by rerunning on the emitted hwnd token
Separate root hwnd from process-owned hwnd; process identity comes from the matched proc/domain/cmd/title/cls/file not the host shell
Prefer focusless tiers first: UIA Invoke/Value/Toggle/Select then Win32 PostMessage then SendInput only if necessary
For browser targets prefer CSS then CDP then UIA; use the exact hwnd grap from find when the tab or frame matters
When adding operator-friendly options to new skills or commands keep them short and orthogonal: prefer verify audit dry-run review inspect list export import or install over redundant synonyms
For Codex-facing workflows expose the minimum state-changing options needed to keep actions safe and reproducible
Keep the grap short quoted and paste-ready for both Claude and Codex; if ambiguity remains rerun inspect/find before acting

AMBIGUOUS GRAP AUTO-RESOLUTION: action commands (type/click/invoke/...) given an ambiguous grap auto-switch to a11y find first (L1 TargetAmbiguityGuard). [OK] Nms in errors.jsonl fields = find step resolved grap; exit:1 = action after resolution failed. Two independent outcomes recorded together.
Discovery sub-actions (read-only safe): inspect (UIA dump) find (copy-paste TARGET line) windows (enumerate top-level) screenshot (PNG of window) ocr (Tesseract on pixels)

Window-state sub-actions: close (WM_CLOSE focusless) minimize maximize restore focus move resize. Use focus sparingly -- it steals foreground. Use a11y invoke on Close-button control instead of a11y close when close button lives in a nested scope
Invoke/select sub-actions: click (best-tier auto) invoke (pure UIA no focus) toggle expand collapse select scroll. invoke is the safest verb for buttons exposing InvokePattern
Input sub-actions: type (SendKeys + focus) type --hotkey (Hotkey for modifiers e.g. ctrl c) set-value (ValuePattern focusless) set-range (RangeValuePattern focusless)
Read sub-actions: read (UIA Name+Value+HelpText concat) clipboard-read clipboard-write. MFC owner-drawn grids have no UIA cells -- use a11y grid-read clipboard bridge (see a11y-grid-read skill)
Wait sub-action: wait --condition enabled
visible
invisible
text --not --timeout N. Poll-based. Useful between state-change actions to avoid races
Common flags across actions: --all (fan-out to every match) --nth N (disambiguate hits) --force (bypass ambiguity guard) --timeout N (per-op cap) --eval-js (CDP targets) --speak (TTS on success)
--ocr opt-in: hack and inspect default to CCA+UIA only. --ocr enables Tesseract + Gemini Vision. Saves CPU and avoids CDP focus steal from VisionWorker

kill sub-action: a11y kill <pattern>[/<ancestor>] kills matching process tree. Ancestor constraint prevents mass-kill. CRITICAL on Win11: never taskkill Notepad -- session restore resurrects all closed tabs; use a11y close instead
Error expectations: action can exit:1 even after grap resolved. Check errors.jsonl fields [OK] for resolution step separately from action step

Action ladder per tier: (1) UIA pattern Invoke/Value/Toggle/Select focusless (2) Win32 PostMessage WM_COMMAND/WM_SETTEXT focusless (3) SendInput keystroke with EnsureFocus -- requires foreground. Most HTS MFC controls skip tier 1 and land on tier 2 directly
author: claude
